title: Ykrul — Wolf Standards, Winter Camps
label: chap:ykrul

overview.tagline: The steppe owes nothing. The wolf pays no debts. Only the fool plays for beauty; the wise play for the exit.
overview.genre: Nomadic epic, pragmatic cunning, wind-scoured honor, Scythian steppe.
overview.starting_location: A winter camp on the steppe edge, banners snapping in frost-bright wind as the Khagan's fires burn low in the east. A Khatun of the Ring offers a clay cup of salted tea. 'The Khagan's kin ride from the salt pan. The old sayings say the straight road is a lie. You came by the bent path—the wolf knows you. Speak your errand before the wind changes. Trade is welcome; lies are tallied.'

lore.history: The Ykrul ride the endless Violet Steppe, where the horizon drinks the sky and survival is a game played without mercy. They are a pragmatic people—harder and more practical than the settled folk of the south. Sentiment is a waste of breath; beauty is a fool's consolation. Their philosophy is not written in books but scratched in the dirt boards of Kon'reh, a game of encirclement, escape, and blockade played with pebbles and sheep-bones. Every child learns before they can hunt: count your exits before you enter, never commit to a single path, and an ally untested is a blade without a sheath. This is not mysticism. It is arithmetic. The steppe is a hard teacher, and the Ykrul are its starkest students. What outsiders call 'geometry' the Ykrul call the Count—the simple, bone-deep understanding that every route has a price, every camp has a weakness, and every alliance is only as strong as the remounts that sustain it. Their law lives in saddlebags. Every crossing has its toll, every ford its depth, every feud a reckoning measured in horse-lengths and heartbeats. Hosts gather by the thousand, then scatter at a word; one night you share their fires, the next they test your bonds. Their banners are wolves' teeth, their treaties braided cords, their memory longer than the rivers that cut the grass. The old folk still recite the Thirty-Six Sayings of the Road, but the wisest know the thirty-seventh is the one you learn alone, on a night when the stars blink out and the wolf does not howl.
lore.first_notice: The smell of horse sweat and woodsmoke. The way every rider counts hoofbeats under their breath. The silence when a stranger approaches the fire. The cold assessment in every eye—not hostile, merely reckoning value.
lore.rule_that_kills: Never ride between two wolves at dusk. The space between them is a snare with no exit. In Kon'reh it is called the Closed Circle; in life it is called death.

entanglements.southeast: Aeler — The mountain passes are sealed by dwarvish tariffs and breath-counters; the Khagan's word is the only passport. Iron flows south; grain flows north when the snows permit.
entanglements.southwest: Vilikari / Acasia — The Foedus Stone and the grass-lands are the eternal negotiation; raids settle what treaties cannot. The hostage strings are the true currency of the border.
entanglements.south: Ecktoria — Legates tally supplies as Ykrul riders stand, silhouettes across the sea of grass.
entanglements.north: The Iron Grass (Kuvani) — Remount clans and rival khagans watch the horizon; alliances shift with the seasons. The wind carries the names of the dead; the dead carry the grudges.

quotes.speaker: Khatun Bortai of the Ring (Elite)
quotes.text: We do not dream on the steppe. We count. We measure breath, grain, and the length of a blade. My grandfather taught me Kon'reh before I could hunt: the board is dirt, the pieces are sheep-bones, and the lesson is always the same—the game ends when one side has no roads left. Politics is merely the same game played with living pieces. To lead here is to know when to trade, when to raid, and when to ride away. Sentiment is a luxury of farmers. We are the children of the horse, the wind, and the knife.

quotes.speaker: Herd-Scout Sogata (Commoner)
quotes.text: Kon'reh teaches that the straight road is a lie. The wise shepherd knows the river bends not from weakness, but from wisdom. We are practical folk. A horse that cannot carry you is meat. An oath that cannot be kept is smoke. Speak true, ride steady, and always count your exits before you enter. The steppe does not forgive the beautiful mistake—only the useful truth.

ninth_taboo.title: The Ninth Taboo Manifestation
ninth_taboo.description: In any Ykrul camp, the ninth cup poured at a feast is for the ancestors. The wise pour eight and leave the ninth as a pause. Those who pour the ninth cup find that the ancestors drink—and the ancestors are always thirsty. The next morning, the pourer's shadow is one inch shorter. If the same person pours the ninth cup nine times, their shadow vanishes entirely, and they become a ghost rider, forever counting cups at the edge of the firelight.

npcs.name: Khatun Bortai
npcs.motivation: Keep the clans fed and mounted; let poets starve
npcs.quirk: Her hostage strings are braided from the hair of oath-breakers.

npcs.name: Noyan Temur
npcs.motivation: Win a winter pasture without losing a single man to talk
npcs.quirk: He has never lost a negotiation. He has never needed to speak.

npcs.name: Sogata
npcs.motivation: Read the weather before the storm because a wet horse is a dead horse
npcs.quirk: He can read a trail by the way the grass bends—and a liar by the way a rider checks his exits.

npcs.name: Elder Saruul
npcs.motivation: Keep the names of the dead so debts are not forgotten
npcs.quirk: She sings the names of the departed each dawn; the dead sing back.

npcs.name: The Dust Prince
npcs.role: Raider who rides the wind's lee
npcs.motivation: Amass enough salt to buy a clan because gold is too heavy to run with
npcs.quirk: He offers escort braids for secrets, never seen from the front.

npcs.name: Storm-Singer Urum
npcs.motivation: Sell weather on credit because even the sky is a resource
npcs.quirk: His bills come due during the Wolf Moon.

places.title: Wolf Milepost
places.subtitle: [TRAVEL]
places.description: Stacked stones; wind whines through eye-holes.
places.flavor: Each stone is a tally. Remove one, and the wind names you debtor.
places.mechanical_hook: A successful Wits+Lore (DV 4) reads the tally; removing a stone grants a safe passage but advances the Feud Spark timer.

places.title: Remount Station
places.subtitle: [TRAVEL]
places.description: Low corral with stamped snow and tether-posts.
places.flavor: The tether-posts are carved with the names of dead horses. The living ones remember.
places.mechanical_hook: Swap mounts for 1 Supply; the station master may demand a story.

places.title: Birch Windbreak
places.subtitle: [RITUAL]
places.description: Above black-earth tracks; old offerings in the bark.
places.flavor: The offerings are strips of wool. Each strip is a prayer for safe crossing.
places.mechanical_hook: Leave a strip of wool to gain +1 die on the next navigation roll.

places.title: Salt Pan
places.subtitle: [WEIGHT]
places.description: Crusted white; hoof-prints like stars across the void.
places.flavor: The pan has a name: 'The Khagan's Mirror.' Look into it, and you see what you owe.
places.mechanical_hook: A successful Spirit+Lore (DV 4) reveals a hidden debt; failure marks you for a feud.

places.title: Reed Ford
places.subtitle: [TRAVEL]
places.description: River braids; poles mark yesterday's safe line.
places.flavor: The poles shift in the night. The river's path changes with the moon.
places.mechanical_hook: Crossing requires a successful Wits+Survival (DV 4); failure means the party is swept downstream and loses 1 Supply.

places.title: Trading Palisade
places.subtitle: [TRADE]
places.flavor: The skulls face outward. They watch for liars and bad weights.
places.mechanical_hook: Trade here is safe (no +DV for social rolls) but the palisade is watched by the Dust Prince's scouts.

places.title: Winter Camp
places.subtitle: [SOCIAL]
places.description: Felt tents in a horseshoe facing the wind.
places.flavor: The open end is for guests. The closed end is for the ancestors.
places.mechanical_hook: Guest-right lasts three nights; a successful Presence+Sway (DV 4) can extend it.

places.title: Kurgan Field
places.subtitle: [DEATH]
places.description: Low barrows; horse-bones bead the grass like prayer beads.
places.flavor: The bones were placed by a bone-singer. Each bead is a name.
places.mechanical_hook: Sleeping here may invite a Spirit encounter; a successful Resolve (DV 4) avoids nightmares.

places.title: Watch Kopje
places.subtitle: [WEATHER]
places.description: Lonely tor with signal fire-scar; the eye of the steppe.
places.flavor: The fire-scar is from a signal that saved a host. The smoke still smells of hope.
places.mechanical_hook: Lighting a fire here gives a +1 die to navigation but alerts all nearby clans.

places.title: Pontoon Crossing
places.subtitle: [TRAVEL]
places.description: Laced hides creak; the current speaks fast and hungry.
places.flavor: The hides are from horses that died crossing. Their ghosts still pull.
places.mechanical_hook: Crossing requires a successful Wits+Ride (DV 5); failure may result in losing 1 Supply to the river.

places.title: Council Hollow
places.subtitle: [SOCIAL]
places.description: Standards planted; the ground tamped like a war-drum.
places.flavor: The standards have ears. Speak a treason here, and every clan will know by dawn.
places.mechanical_hook: Any promise made here is binding; breaking it advances the Hostage Call timer.

places.title: Khagan's Station
places.subtitle: [AUTHORITY]
places.description: Broad ger on a timber platform; guards in silence.
places.flavor: The guards never blink. They are paid in salt and secrets.
places.mechanical_hook: An audience with the Khagan requires a token or a tribute (1 Supply).

places.title: Sky Steppe
places.subtitle: [MYSTERY]
places.description: Open, wind like a voice; tracks keep pace beside you.
places.flavor: The tracks are not yours. They belong to the ancestors who ride with you.
places.mechanical_hook: A successful Spirit+Lore (DV 4) may gain guidance from the ancestors; failure may result in a Sky Omen.

people_and_factions.title: Herd-Scout
people_and_factions.subtitle: [TRAVEL]
people_and_factions.description: Three ponies and six opinions; the horizon as a map.
people_and_factions.flavor: He can read a trail by the way the grass bends. He can read a liar by the way a rider checks his exits.
people_and_factions.what_they_carry: Can guide the party for 1 Supply.
people_and_factions.what_they_ask: A story from beyond the steppe.

people_and_factions.title: Camp-Mother
people_and_factions.subtitle: [SOCIAL]
people_and_factions.description: Chooses where fires live; hospitality is law.
people_and_factions.flavor: She has never drawn a blade. She has never needed to.
people_and_factions.what_they_carry: Can grant a Camp Token (Diamond) for a favor.
people_and_factions.what_they_ask: A promise to respect the fire.

people_and_factions.title: Banner Youth
people_and_factions.subtitle: [COMBAT]
people_and_factions.description: Minding the wolves' tooth standard; pride as duty.
people_and_factions.flavor: The standard is older than his grandmother's grandmother. He will die before it falls.
people_and_factions.what_they_carry: Can fetch messages or small items.
people_and_factions.what_they_ask: A story of a famous battle.

people_and_factions.title: Salt-Broker
people_and_factions.subtitle: [TRADE]
people_and_factions.description: Weighs promises like grain; trade as precision.
people_and_factions.flavor: Her scales are honest—because a dishonest scale is a broken tool, and tools are expensive.
people_and_factions.what_they_carry: Can provide Salt Allotment (Diamond) for 1 Supply.
people_and_factions.what_they_ask: A promise to trade with her clan.

people_and_factions.title: Remount Keeper
people_and_factions.subtitle: [TRAVEL]
people_and_factions.description: String-master with a book of brands; horses as currency.
people_and_factions.flavor: His book is written in hoof-prints. He can read a horse's soul.
people_and_factions.what_they_carry: Can provide a Remount Chit (Diamond) for 1 Supply.
people_and_factions.what_they_ask: A name to add to his book.

people_and_factions.title: Bone-Singer
people_and_factions.subtitle: [DEATH]
people_and_factions.description: Knows the kurgans' proper names; death as memory.
people_and_factions.flavor: She sings the names of the dead each dawn. The dead sing back.
people_and_factions.what_they_carry: Can perform a ritual to gain a Sky Omen (Diamond) for 1 Supply.
people_and_factions.what_they_ask: A name of a forgotten ancestor.

people_and_factions.title: Road-Judge
people_and_factions.subtitle: [LAW]
people_and_factions.description: Tümen scribe with seals and a patient ear.
people_and_factions.flavor: His seals are from three empires. He uses whichever one is still valid.
people_and_factions.what_they_carry: Can provide a Foedus Seal (Diamond) for 1 Supply.
people_and_factions.what_they_ask: A correct reading of a treaty.

people_and_factions.title: Noyan Envoy
people_and_factions.subtitle: [SOCIAL]
people_and_factions.description: Silver paiza, iron smile; diplomacy as a blade.
people_and_factions.flavor: His paiza was a gift from a Khagan who is now dead. He does not mention that.
people_and_factions.what_they_carry: Can arrange a meeting with a banner-lord.
people_and_factions.what_they_ask: A show of respect to the Khagan.

people_and_factions.title: Host Captain
people_and_factions.subtitle: [COMBAT]
people_and_factions.description: The tent-wall is a map; strategy in every fold.
people_and_factions.flavor: He can see a battle in the way smoke rises. He is usually right.
people_and_factions.what_they_carry: Can command a small force for one scene (Cap 2).

people_and_factions.title: Falcon Courier
people_and_factions.subtitle: [TRAVEL]
people_and_factions.description: Leather gauntlets, answers sooner than asked.
people_and_factions.flavor: The falcon is a sleeper agent. It belongs to a different clan. The courier doesn't know.

people_and_factions.title: Khatun of Ring
people_and_factions.subtitle: [LAW]
people_and_factions.description: Keeper of camp-law and hostage strings.
people_and_factions.flavor: Her strings are braided from the hair of hostages. She knows each strand's owner.
people_and_factions.what_they_carry: Can grant a Ring Audience (Diamond) for a service.
people_and_factions.what_they_ask: A hostage string or a blood-oath.

people_and_factions.title: Khagan's Kin
people_and_factions.subtitle: [SOCIAL]
people_and_factions.description: A nephew or niece sent to bind a foedus with a look.
people_and_factions.flavor: They have never lost a negotiation. They have never needed to speak.
people_and_factions.what_they_carry: Can provide a Khagan's Writ (Diamond) for a significant service.
people_and_factions.what_they_ask: A favor to be named later.

people_and_factions.title: Sky-Speaker
people_and_factions.subtitle: [MAGIC]
people_and_factions.description: Shaman of storms; shortcuts owe them a price.
people_and_factions.flavor: They can call lightning—or call it off. The price is always a story.
people_and_factions.what_they_carry: Can provide a Sky's Exception (Diamond) for a story.
people_and_factions.what_they_ask: A true name of a storm.

complications.title: White Squall
complications.subtitle: [WEATHER]
complications.description: Dry snow, lost horizons; navigation by gut and horse sense.
complications.flavor: The snow tastes of iron. Someone is bleeding nearby.
complications.mechanical_hook: Navigation is Desperate; a successful Wits+Survival (DV 5) keeps the party on course. Failure may separate the party.

complications.title: Rasputitsa
complications.subtitle: [WEATHER]
complications.description: The road becomes porridge; wheels are lies on the earth.
complications.flavor: The mud has a smell—old blood and older lies.
complications.mechanical_hook: Travel at half speed; a failed Endurance test (DV 4) results in 1 Fatigue.

complications.title: Remount Sickness
complications.subtitle: [DEATH]
complications.description: A cough in the string; swaps get awkward, trust thinner.
complications.flavor: The cough is a curse. The horses know who cast it.
complications.mechanical_hook: A horse goes lame; lose 1 Supply to replace it or risk a remount post.

complications.title: Salt Shortage
complications.subtitle: [WEIGHT]
complications.description: Meat spoils; tempers thin like winter air.
complications.flavor: The salt was cut with grave dust. Someone wanted the winter to be hard.
complications.mechanical_hook: Supplies cost double; a successful Wits+Trade (DV 4) finds an alternative source.

complications.title: Hostage Call
complications.subtitle: [SOCIAL]
complications.description: Protocol invoked; someone must sit in the tent.
complications.flavor: The tent has no floor. The hostage can see the earth beneath—and the bones.
complications.mechanical_hook: A member of the party must offer themselves as a hostage or find a token (Diamond) to substitute.

complications.title: Feud Spark
complications.subtitle: [WEIGHT]
complications.description: A name spoken wrong reopens an old debt; blood is the only coin that pays.
complications.flavor: The name was spelled correctly. The insult was in the pause before it.
complications.mechanical_hook: A blood-feud begins; the party must pay a wergild (2 Supply) or face a raid.

complications.title: Grassfire
complications.subtitle: [DISASTER]
complications.description: Runs with the wind faster than horses; escape as a prayer.
complications.flavor: The fire has a face. It is the face of a Khagan who burned a village.
complications.mechanical_hook: The party must flee; a successful Ride test (DV 5) escapes, failure costs 1 Supply.

complications.title: Foedus Recall
complications.subtitle: [LAW]
complications.description: Treaty terms re-read; your papers disagree.
complications.flavor: The treaty was written on both sides. The other side used a different ink.
complications.mechanical_hook: A legal dispute; a successful Wits+Lore (DV 4) finds the loophole, or a duel settles it.

complications.title: River Break
complications.subtitle: [TRAVEL]
complications.description: Ice goes; ferries don't; crossing becomes a gamble.
complications.flavor: The ice is still thick in the middle. The edges are lying.
complications.mechanical_hook: Crossing is a Risk; a failed Body+Athletics (DV 5) may result in a lost horse or gear.

complications.title: Raid Shadow
complications.subtitle: [COMBAT]
complications.description: Another banner follows at a patient distance.
complications.flavor: They are not attacking. They are waiting for you to make a mistake.
complications.mechanical_hook: A raiding party stalks the party; a successful Wits+Scout (DV 4) evades them.

complications.title: Kurultai Call
complications.subtitle: [SOCIAL]
complications.flavor: The kurultai has a list of grievances. Your name is near the top.
complications.mechanical_hook: The party is summoned to a council; a successful Presence+Sway (DV 5) can avoid a feud.

complications.title: Wolf Muster
complications.subtitle: [AUTHORITY]
complications.description: A levy raised; every road a checkpoint, every rider a judge.
complications.flavor: The muster horn is a wolf's skull. It sounds like a scream.
complications.mechanical_hook: The party must provide 1 Supply per person or be conscripted; a successful Sway test (DV 5) may secure an exemption.

complications.title: Sky Omen
complications.subtitle: [OMEN]
complications.description: Three suns, or none; routes rewrite themselves.
complications.flavor: The omen is a wedge of cranes flying north in winter. The sky is wrong.
complications.mechanical_hook: Navigation is Desperate for the scene; a successful Spirit+Lore (DV 4) may reveal a hidden path.

rewards.title: Camp Token
rewards.subtitle: [SOCIAL]
rewards.description: One night's lawful fire and fodder; hospitality as a right.
rewards.flavor: The token is a horsehair braid. Burn it to claim your fire.
rewards.cost: The token is consumed on use; the host will expect a story in return.

rewards.title: Salt Allotment
rewards.subtitle: [TRADE]
rewards.description: Draw from a named store without quarrel.
rewards.flavor: The allotment is a salt-cake. Break it in half to share.
rewards.cost: Using it may anger other claimants; a Hostage Call may follow.

rewards.title: Ford-Right
rewards.subtitle: [TRAVEL]
rewards.description: Cross a named braid at lawful depth.
rewards.flavor: The right is a pole. Plant it in the river, and the ford will hold—once.
rewards.cost: The pole is single-use; the ford's depth may change seasonally.

rewards.title: Remount Chit
rewards.subtitle: [TRAVEL]
rewards.description: Swap two tired mounts for fresh.
rewards.flavor: The chit is a horse's tooth. The remount keeper will not smile when you present it.
rewards.cost: The chit is for a specific remount station; it may not be honored elsewhere.

rewards.title: Escort Braid
rewards.subtitle: [TRAVEL]
rewards.description: Two riders see you through a bad stretch.
rewards.flavor: The braid is woven from the riders' own hair. They will die before they cut it.
rewards.cost: The escort lasts one leg of travel; they may have other obligations.

rewards.title: Safe-Hostage
rewards.subtitle: [SOCIAL]
rewards.description: Place or take token-kin for a truce.
rewards.flavor: The hostage is a child. The child is patient.
rewards.cost: The hostage must be returned; failure to do so starts a Hostage Call.

rewards.title: Paiza Tablet
rewards.subtitle: [AUTHORITY]
rewards.description: Priority on the Wolf Road and at posts.
rewards.flavor: The tablet is bronze. It is warm when your reputation is good.
rewards.cost: If the tablet is challenged and found invalid, it may be confiscated.

rewards.title: Foedus Seal
rewards.subtitle: [LAW]
rewards.description: A treaty clause that trumps petty order.
rewards.flavor: The seal is a wolf's head. Press it into wax, and the steppe listens.
rewards.cost: The seal is single-use; using it may attract the Khagan's attention.

rewards.title: Market-Green
rewards.subtitle: [TRADE]
rewards.description: Trade under a banner; weapons sheathed.
rewards.flavor: The banner is a green field. No blood may be spilled beneath it.
rewards.cost: The banner protects you only at the market; raiders may wait outside.

rewards.title: Standard Guard
rewards.subtitle: [COMBAT]
rewards.flavor: The standard is a wolf's tooth on a pole. Raiders will bow—or die.
rewards.cost: The standard identifies you as a friend of a specific clan; its enemies may become your enemies.

rewards.title: Ring Audience
rewards.subtitle: [SOCIAL]
rewards.description: The Khatun hears you alone; precedent sticks.
rewards.flavor: The audience is held at midnight. The fire is the only witness.
rewards.cost: The Khatun will expect a service in return; refusing may be dangerous.

rewards.title: Khagan's Writ
rewards.subtitle: [AUTHORITY]
rewards.description: Doors open, mouths close across the steppe.
rewards.flavor: The writ is a sheet of hammered silver. It bends, but it does not break.
rewards.cost: Using the writ may anger rivals of the Khagan; a Feud Spark may follow.

rewards.title: Sky's Exception
rewards.subtitle: [WEATHER]
rewards.description: Once, the weather ignores you (storm, whiteout, dust).
rewards.flavor: The exception is a single breath. Use it well.
rewards.cost: The exception is single-use; the sky will remember your face.

quick_use_notes.draw_rules: Draw until you have all four suits: Spade = place, Heart = actor, Club = pressure, Diamond = leverage. Highest rank sets the main Timer (2–5 → 4, 6–10 → 6, J/Q/K → 8, A → 10).
quick_use_notes.diamonds: Diamonds are codified outcomes (passes/remounts/truces) that change position rather than call for a roll.
quick_use_notes.ace_rule: If any Ace appears, echo steppe and sky motifs—wind that speaks, horses that remember, treaties that bind across generations, and the cold arithmetic of Kon'reh made manifest.

additional_features.name: Kon'reh — The Game of the Steppe
additional_features.description: The Ykrul do not speak of 'geometry.' They speak of Kon'reh, a dirt-board game of encirclement, escape, and blockade played with pebbles and bone. Every Ykrul child learns its lessons: count your exits before you enter; never commit to a single path; a screen of light pieces can trap a heavy king; the best move is the one your opponent thinks you have forgotten. In Ykrul culture, Kon'reh is not recreation—it is education. A war-council may be fought out on a Kon'reh board before a single arrow is nocked. A treaty may be argued as a series of moves and counter-moves. When a Ykrul says 'the Count,' they mean the cold reckoning of advantage: remounts, routes, grain-days, and the price of a blade in winter. In play: When a Ykrul NPC evaluates an offer or a threat, they are playing Kon'reh in their head. Social rolls against Ykrul suffer +1 DV unless the player can demonstrate a 'route of escape' (an exit, a concession, or a hostage) that makes the deal survivable for both sides.

additional_features.name: Hostage Strings [4]
additional_features.description: Every oath is secured with a hostage string—a token kin, retainer, or sworn ally left in the other camp. If the deal is broken, the hostage's fate becomes the story's next beat. Mechanically: create a 4-segment Hostage timer for any major treaty. On breach, the timer becomes a countdown to vengeance. Ykrul take hostages as a matter of course; refusing to give one is treated as a declaration of bad faith.

additional_features.name: Steppe Law
additional_features.description: Travelers are bound by steppe law: hospitality lasts three nights, insults last three generations. The Keeper may demand 1 SB when either law is broken. The three nights are measured by the Camp-Mother's fire; the three generations are measured by the Bone-Singer's memory.

additional_features.name: Sky Omens
additional_features.description: When the sky shows strange signs—mock-suns, green fire, thunder without storm—the steppe itself speaks. Once per scene, players may treat an omen as a Diamond, shifting Position without a roll. The GM may bank 1 SB for future weather complications. Pragmatic Ykrul treat omens as weather-data misread by the fearful; even so, they do not ride against them.

additional_features.name: Bowl and Board
additional_features.description: Ykrul elders settle disputes not by shouting but by drawing two circles in the dirt: the Bowl for fairness, then the Board for routes and concessions. A successful Wits + Lore (or relevant skill) roll creates a Seasonal Concession String at a ford, harbor, or pass. On a miss, the Market Moot [4] timer advances as factions argue.

additional_features.name: Local Patrons
additional_features.description: The Hoof — The Traveler. The path that never ends, the remount that never tires. The Wolf's Tooth — The Witness. Every oath is counted. Every broken promise is a scar. The Salt Throat — Maelstraeus. The weight that salts your tongue; the interest that blinds your horses. The Sky Father — Kuva. The wind that carries names; the storm that erases them.

additional_features.name: The Thirty-Seven Sayings of the Road (Excerpts)
additional_features.description: Let the river freeze before you cross. Borrow a corridor to trap a king. Loot a burning house. Hide your dagger behind a smile. The reed bends, the oak breaks. A distant banner casts no shadow. The guest who outstays his welcome steals the host's peace. Count exits, not victims.

additional_features.name: Pursuit Ladder (Near / Mid / Far)
additional_features.description: Advance/Withdraw (Wits + Ride): On a hit, shift one band. On a strong hit, also impose Dust Cloud. Cut the Herd (Presence + Sway): On a hit, freeze range for one exchange; if a remount chit is in your possession, +1 die. Weather the Squall (Endurance + Survival): On a hit in White Squall, you pick who keeps their bearings; on a miss, the GM does.

additional_features.name: Terrain Stock (d20)
additional_features.description: A table of striking details to dress a hex of the Violet Steppe, including wolf mileposts, reed ford poles, fallen standards, pony skull circles, and other evocative landmarks.

additional_features.name: Lair Seeds (One-Page Rides)
additional_features.description: A dozen lair seeds for Ykrul encounters, including Ger of Echoes, Salt-Pit Shrine, Remount Vault, Kite-Maker's Bluff, Storm-Rider's Stone, Frozen Court, Banner Grave, String-Mother's Yard, Fire-Under-Snow, Quiet Palisade, Ashen Trident, and Gray Ger.

additional_features.name: Steppe Bestiary (Palette)
additional_features.description: Creatures of the Violet Steppe: Steppe Manticores (bone quills, raid-song), Dust Hulks (wind-packed silhouettes, water offering), Bone Kites (rune-etched carrion birds, name-stake barter), Frost Wargs (blue-pelt wolves, warm milk crossing), Reed Stalkers (marsh hunters, reed truce), Salt Screamers (pale eels, ash scattering).

additional_features.name: Relic & Find (d10)
additional_features.description: A table of kurgan caches and lost hoards, including wolf-tooth standard tips, bronze paiza halves, salt-needles, frost-bitten stirrups, braid-beads, river-depth rods, iron hoof-bells, bone-lot cups, wind-wrung scarves, and wolf-road tablet scraps.

additional_features.name: Complication Twists (d8)
additional_features.description: Twists that deepen travel complications: White Squall reveals a remount vault; Rasputitsa exposes kurgan stones; Feud Spark was staged by a salt-broker; Hostage Call is for a Safe-Hostage you already carry; Grassfire runs uphill toward a council hollow; Foedus Recall cites a river you crossed yesterday; Raid Shadow is your own tracks; Kurultai Call stacks councils.

additional_features.name: Boss Seeds (Three-Phase Foes)
additional_features.description: The String-Mother (Nine Hostages) — Phases: Knot → Tighten → Sever. Cracks: return a hostage string, win a Bowl and Board arbitration, cut the right knot. The Dust Prince (Rider of the Red Wind) — Phases: Raid Shadow → Salt Tithe → Kurultai Coup. Cracks: spill a salt allotment in his path, unmask his face before the council, outride him through a white squall.

eastern_steppe.title: Ykrul of the Eastern Steppe — The Iron Grass Clans
eastern_steppe.description: The wind is colder here, and so are the hearts. We do not forget an insult because we cannot afford to. The grass is thinner, the wolves are bolder, and the sky speaks only in threats. The western Ykrul trade in stories. We trade in teeth.
eastern_steppe.quote: The westerners count their weights in silk and verses. We count ours in frost and iron. A broken oath here is not a story for the skald—it is a hole in the winter larder. We do not forgive because we cannot. The Hollow rides with us every night. We have no room for mercy, and even less for poetry. Kon'reh is still our teacher, but the board is frozen, and the pieces are bones.

clan_trait.name: Frost-Bitten
clan_trait.description: The clan survives on the edge of the permafrost. +1 die to Survival in cold weather, but –1 die to all social rolls with lowlanders (they stink of frozen horse).

clan_trait.name: Kuvani-Kin
clan_trait.description: This clan has traded with the Kuvani for generations. They speak the Kuvani tongue and may call on one Kuvani remount station per journey. Rivals whisper they have forgotten the old ways.

clan_trait.name: Wolf-Pacted
clan_trait.description: The clan has a standing truce with a local wolf pack. Wolves do not attack their herds. In exchange, the clan leaves every ninth calf unbranded—for the wolves. The pack sometimes fights beside them in battle (Cap 2, appears once per arc).

clan_trait.name: Salt-Misers
clan_trait.description: This clan controls a small, bitter salt pan. They trade salt for iron with the Kuvani. They are wealthy but paranoid; any visitor is assumed to be a spy. +1 die to detect deception, but starting Position in parley is Desperate.

clan_trait.name: Curse-Bearers
clan_trait.description: A broken oath from three generations ago still hangs over the clan. Their horses are prone to sickness, and their children are born with gray eyes. They seek a way to lift the curse—and will do almost anything to find it.

clan_trait.name: Road-Breakers
clan_trait.description: This clan claims the right to open or close the high passes east. They carry a brass key (actually a bronze paiza) that Kuvani caravans recognize. They are isolationist, proud, and quick to draw steel over matters of passage.

hazards.name: Frost-Rime Grass
hazards.description: The grass is coated in a crystalline frost that cuts horse legs. Travel speed halved for one leg. On a failed Survival roll, one mount goes lame (mark 1 Fatigue or lose 1 Supply).

hazards.name: Wolf Howl at Dusk
hazards.description: A pack shadows the party for a day and a night. They will not attack unless the party is weak or separated. If a character sleeps alone, roll Spirit+Resolve (DV 3) or be found by wolves at midnight.

hazards.name: Ice Lens Mirage
hazards.description: The flat steppe plays tricks. Navigation rolls suffer –1 die for the leg. On a miss, the party wanders into a kurgan field—the dead are restless. Start a Spirit Attention [4] timer.

hazards.name: Salt Pan Crack
hazards.description: A dried salt pan has a hidden fissure. A horse or wagon breaks through. Lose 1 Supply (salt pork ruined) and mark 1 Fatigue for the party as they pull the animal free.

hazards.name: Kuvani Border Markers
hazards.description: The party has crossed into a Kuvani grazing ground without permission. Kuvani riders appear (Cap 2, non-hostile unless provoked). They demand a story or a small offering of salt. Refusal starts a Border Dispute [4] timer.

hazards.name: Silent Sky
hazards.description: No birds, no wind, no insect sounds. The Hollow is near. For the next scene, any roll that shows a 1 also summons a Oath-Keeper—it will appear at the end of the scene to ask one question. Answer poorly, and it takes a memory.

encounter.name: Hungry Hosts
encounter.description: The camp is low on supplies. They will trade—at ruinous prices. But they will also remember a fair deal. If you overpay (give 1 extra Supply), gain a String (Eastern Ykrul favor).

encounter.name: Grieving Clan
encounter.description: A child died last night. The camp is in mourning. They will not trade or talk. If you offer a true story about a lost loved one, they will share a meal.

encounter.name: Feud-Watch
encounter.description: The clan is on alert for raiders from a rival banner. They mistake the party for scouts. The first social roll is at Desperate Position. A successful Deception or Bribe (cost 1 Boon or 1 Supply) clears the misunderstanding.

encounter.name: Salt-Broker's Caravan
encounter.description: A small caravan of salt traders, heavily armed. They are willing to trade salt for news of the west. They ask pointed questions about the Violet Steppe—they are planning a raid.

encounter.name: Kurgan Pilgrims
encounter.description: A group of bone-singers and their guards are traveling to a sacred kurgan. They will allow the party to accompany them if the party helps perform a ritual: each member must whisper a forgotten name into a horse skull. Afterwards, the party gains +1 die to resist fear for one day, but the Hollow knows one of your names.

encounter.name: Storm-Singer's Challenge
encounter.description: A sky-speaker challenges the party to a weather-calling contest. Roll Spirit+Lore vs. DV 4. On a success, the storm-singer is impressed and grants a Sky's Exception (ignore one weather hazard). On a failure, the party is drenched and loses 1 Fatigue.

encounter.name: Hollow-Touched Camp
encounter.description: The camp is silent. Everyone is sitting still, staring at a single point. A Oath-Keeper has passed through. If the party can speak the name of the broken oath, the camp will wake. Otherwise, they must leave an offering of salt and retreat.

encounter.name: Iron Grass Market
encounter.description: A rare gathering of three clans at a neutral ford. The market lasts only one day. Goods for sale: remounts (cost 1 Supply each), salt (1 Supply per 2 portions), iron arrowheads (1 Boon for 1 die bonus on next ranged attack), and one cursed paiza (half price, but the previous owner's ghost follows).

customs.name: No Left Hand
customs.description: Offering anything with the left hand is an insult. All social rolls using Presence+Sway suffer –1 die unless you explicitly use your right hand.

customs.name: Fire First, Talk Later
customs.description: Guests must warm their hands at the central fire before speaking. If you refuse, the host will be offended—the first request you make is automatically refused.

customs.name: The Empty Saddle
customs.description: One saddle in the camp is always empty. It belongs to a rider who died last winter. If you sit in it, you must tell a story about a death you witnessed. If the story is true, the ghost may give you a warning (GM reveals one future hazard). If false, the ghost haunts you for a day (you gain the Haunted condition).

customs.name: No Naming the Dead
customs.description: Eastern Ykrul do not speak the names of the deceased for one year after death. If you accidentally name a dead person, the clan demands a blood-price: 1 Supply or a lock of your hair.

customs.name: Salt Before Meat
customs.description: A meal is not blessed until the host throws a pinch of salt into the fire. If you eat before the salt, you are considered an oath-breaker. The meal will not nourish you (you do not recover Fatigue from that rest).

customs.name: The Ninth Breath
customs.description: When drinking fermented mare's milk, you must pause after the eighth sip. If you take a ninth, you must immediately name an enemy. The clan will consider that enemy as their own for one season. The enemy may not appreciate this.

superstitions.name: Iron to the Frost
superstitions.effect: Press a cold iron nail into the ground before a white squall. Ignore the first Frost-Rime Grass penalty.

superstitions.name: Braid the Tail
superstitions.effect: Tie a single knot in a horse's tail hair. Cancel Wolf Howl at Dusk or take –1 die on your next survival roll (your choice).

superstitions.name: Name to the Hollow
superstitions.effect: Whisper a dead enemy's name into a salt pan at midnight. Gain +1 Effect when evading Oath-Keepers this scene, but mark Obligation to the Eastern Khatun.

boss_seeds.name: The Frost-Khagan (Undying)
cracks: burn a horse tooth in a fire
speak his true name (carved on his saddle)
offer a living rider to take his place

boss_seeds.name: The Salt-Throat (Cursed Broker)
cracks: pay a wergild in untainted salt
cut his hostage string with a silver knife

boss_seeds.name: The Hollow's Rider (Oath-Keeper in Flesh)
cracks: feed him a memory of warmth
show him a red thread tied in eight knots
outride him to the horizon—the Hollow cannot cross moving water

boss_seeds.name: The Iron Grandmother (Bone-Singer Revenant)
cracks: sing a true name she does not know
answer her riddle: 'What weight is never paid?'

boss_seeds.name: The Wolf-Banner (Pack-Leader)
cracks: offer a raw heart to his pack

boss_seeds.name: The Sky-Weeping Child (Curse Bearer)
cracks: warm her hands with your own breath
speak her true name (her mother is still alive—she will tell you)
find the horse that was born at the same moment—it carries the other half of the curse

at_a_glance.core_traits: Isolationist, pragmatic, harsher honor code, live among Kuvani but do not intermarry.
at_a_glance.key_resources: Salt, iron, remounts, wolf truces, kurgan spirits.
at_a_glance.common_conflicts: Feuds over salt pans, disputes with Kuvani over grazing, Hollow incursions in winter, curse-lifting quests.
at_a_glance.tone: Bleaker than western Ykrul. Fewer stories, more teeth. Hospitality is still sacred—but the guest is expected to earn it.
at_a_glance.ritual_objects: Frost-bitten stirrups, bronze paizas, horse-tooth chits, salt-cakes, wolf skull standards.

lore_echoes.title: The Bitter Root
lore_echoes.description: Daughters of the Cord run a hospice near the salt pans. They ask no payment—but they cut threads. A renegade Sister hides among the herders, trying to untie her cord from a Ykrul blood-weight.

lore_echoes.title: The Threshold Compendium
lore_echoes.description: The kurgans are Class II Anchor sites—the spirits of old Khagans bound by unfinished wergild. Saikou Ira would carry a bag of salt in each hand here.

lore_echoes.title: Malachai, the Chained Angel
lore_echoes.description: A silver coin that never spends circulates among the salt-brokers. Take it, and your store never runs dry—but the coin will reappear in the salt pan at every new moon.

lore_echoes.title: The Gray Wanderer's Grimoire
lore_echoes.description: Ykrul psions are called 'Weather-Readers.' They taste the future in the wind. Road-judges employ two—and have never trusted a verdict they didn't hear from the sky.

superstitions.name: Salt to the Wind
superstitions.effect: Toss a pinch into the air before a long ride. Ignore the first White Squall penalty.

superstitions.name: Knot the String
superstitions.effect: Tie a single knot in a hostage cord. Cancel Feud Spark or take –1 die on your next oath (your choice).

superstitions.name: Name to the Kurgan
superstitions.effect: Whisper a dead enemy's name into a horse's ear. Gain +1 Effect when raiding their clan this scene, but mark Obligation to the Bone-Singer.

minor_complications: **Exposure:** Road-judges or border riders take notice.
**Noise:** A nearby camp or raiding party is alerted.
**Trace:** Hoofprints or broken grass mark your route for feud-kin.
**Delay:** A horse throws a shoe, a rope breaks—lose time.
**Supply Strain:** Mark +1 segment on fodder or water timer.
moderate_setbacks: **Alarm Raised:** A watch kopje signals; riders are coming.
**Position Lost:** Rasputitsa or white squall hides the trail.
**Foe Appears:** A raid shadow or hostage taker arrives.
**Gear Trouble:** A saddle snaps, a bowstring frays.
**Lock/Barrier:** A river breaks, a ford becomes impassable.
serious_trouble: **Reinforcements:** A wolf muster or kurultai war party arrives.
**Key Gear Breaks:** A horse goes lame, a paiza cracks.
**Major Twist:** A grassfire cuts off retreat, or a feud spark escalates.
**Rail Tick:** Advance a campaign timer (e.g. Hostage Crisis).
**Condition Applied:** Mark Exhausted or Marked by Feud.
major_turns: **Trap Springs:** A hidden kurgan collapses, a pontoon sinks.
**Authority Arrival:** Khatun of the Ring, Khagan's Kin, or Sky-Speaker intervenes.
**Scene Shift:** A sky omen rewrites the terrain; paths appear or vanish.
**Patron Omen:** A wolf watches, a horse speaks, the wind carries a name.
**Narrative Pivot:** The feud is not what it seemed; the hostage was a willing sacrifice.
region_specific: **Steppe Law:** Hospitality fails, insults echo, treaties shift mid-parley.
**Horse Culture:** Mounts refuse to move, remount posts are empty, riding rolls suffer –1 die.
**Sky Omens:** Weather changes instantly, stars point the wrong way, a raven shadows the party.

patronage_and_power.description: In Ykrul society, power flows through the careful management of hospitality, the binding of oaths with hostages, and the ability to navigate the complex web of tribal relationships. The Khagan maintains authority through the loyalty of noyans and the respect of khatuns, while local leaders wield influence through their control of resources like salt, remounts, and safe passage. The true power lies with those who understand the old calculations—the pragmatic reckonings of Kon'reh—and can maintain the delicate balance between hospitality and honor.
for_the_gm: Tie rewards to visible symbols (tokens, braids, standards) that can be challenged, stolen, or voided.
Use the Bowl and Board procedure for disputes—first fairness (Bowl), then routes and concessions (Board), then a seasonal right.
Let rival hosts issue conflicting protections, forcing players to choose whose favor matters more.

Remember that Ykrul pragmatism is colder than southern sentiment. An ally is an asset; a debtor is a liability. Mercy is a luxury, not a virtue. The game of Kon'reh teaches that a trapped piece is dead weight—free it or sacrifice it, but never weep for it.

faction_entanglements: **Khagan's Household:** Respect the Khagan's writ and offer tribute; defiance invites the wolf muster.
**Khatun of the Ring:** Honor the hostage strings and steppe law; break them and become a feud target.
**Salt-Brokers:** Keep trade fair; cheat them and the salt pans will close to you.
**Eastern Ykrul (Iron Grass):** Survive by wit, ride by necessity; trust no one until they have shared salt.
**Kuvani Clans:** They are rivals and occasional trading partners; cross a border marker and face a raid.
cross_regional_ties: **Aeler:** The mountain passes are sealed by tariffs; the Khagan's word is the only passport.
**Vilikari/Acasia:** The Foedus Stone and grass-lands are the eternal negotiation; raids settle what treaties cannot.
**Ecktoria:** Legates tally supplies as Ykrul riders stand silhouettes across the sea of grass.
**Iron Grass (Kuvani):** Remount clans and rival khagans watch the horizon; alliances shift with the seasons.
